title: Connector Configuration Validation Flow
key_interfaces: Field.ValidationOutput: void accept(Field field, Object value, String problemMessage)
Field.Validator: int validate(Configuration config, Field field, ValidationOutput problems)
interaction_flow_path1_kafka_connect_val: Configuration → Field.Set: fields.forEach(field -> put ConfigValue(field.name()) into map)
Field → Field.Validator: typeValidator = validatorForType(type); typeValidator.validate(config, this, problems)
Field → Field: recursively validate dependents via field.dependents(parentValue)
Configuration → KafkaConnect: return Map<String, ConfigValue>  [ConfigValue.errorMessages empty = valid]
interaction_flow_path2_start_time_valida: Field.validate(config, problems) → Field.Validator: typeValidator.validate(config, this, problems)  [count errors]
Field.validate(config, problems) → Field.Validator: customValidator.validate(config, this, problems)  [count errors]
Field.Validator → ValidationOutput: problems.accept(field, value, problemMessage)  [if error]
ValidationOutput → Consumer<String>: format 'The {field} value {val} is invalid: {problem}'
Configuration → Connector: return false if any errors
Connector: throw ConnectException('Error configuring ...; check the logs for details')
interaction_flow_jdbc_sink: Configuration → Field: iterate, run validators per field
JdbcSinkConnectorConfig.validate(): additional check — cannot define both column.exclude.list and column.include.list
built_in_validators: Field.validatorForType(Type) — auto-assigns: isInteger, isLong, isBoolean, isShort, isDouble per Kafka Type
Field.EnumRecommender<T> — validates value is in enum set, also implements ConfigDef.Validator
Field.isClassName — validates Java class name format

error_accumulation.path_map: ConfigValue.addErrorMessage() accumulates multiple errors per field; returned in Map<String,ConfigValue>
error_accumulation.path_record: ValidationOutput.accept() called for each problem; formatted as string, passed to Consumer<String> (e.g. LOGGER::error)
error_accumulation.outcome_valid: return true / return non-empty Map (ConfigValue with no errors)
error_accumulation.outcome_invalid: return false → ConnectException thrown; or Map<String,ConfigValue> with error messages returned to Kafka Connect

connector_rejection_acceptance.kafka_connect_path: Connector.validate() returns Map<String,ConfigValue>; Kafka Connect UI shows field errors. Connector is rejected if ConfigValue contains errorMessages.
connector_rejection_acceptance.start_time_path: Connector.start() calls validateAndRecord(); if false → throw ConnectException → connector fails to start.
connector_rejection_acceptance.jdbc_sink_path: JdbcSinkConnectorConfig.validate() throws ConnectException on invalid config or conflicting include/exclude lists.

important_notes: Field.withValidation(Validator...) chains validators: Validator.and(other) returns (config,field,problems) -> v1.validate() + v2.validate()
Field.Set.forEachTopLevelField skips fields that appear as dependents of other fields — avoids double-validation
Dependent fields validated recursively: parent field drives which dependents are activated based on parent's current value
ALL_FIELDS = Field.setOf(CONFIG_DEFINITION.all()) — static set built at class load time from ConfigDefinition
Passwords are masked in validateAndRecord log output (pattern-matched then replaced with ****)
BinlogConnectorConfig is abstract; concrete subclasses are MySqlConnectorConfig, MariaDbConnectorConfig
